page: Microsoft Azure E48bds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the EBDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at a base frequency of 2.8 GHz, configured with 24 physical cores and 48 dedicated vCPUs. The hardware profile includes 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per-core memory density, alongside 1843 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities.

In benchmark evaluations, this server demonstrates strong multi-core and database capabilities. It achieved a Passmark CPU score of 50758.8 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15896.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Database operations performed at 23567.62 on the Passmark scale, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reached 221.85 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core performance and memory write bandwidth fall into the average category, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1689.0 and large-block memory write speeds of 65.66 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of this configuration is its lack of hardware acceleration. Without a dedicated GPU, LLM text generation for large models drops to 2.33 tokens/sec. The server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, and multi-threaded data processing pipelines that can leverage the high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local storage capacity without requiring GPU resources.
description: Microsoft Azure E48bds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EBDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM and 1843 GB of local storage. The server demonstrates strong multi-core and database performance, highlighted by a Passmark CPU score of 50758.8 and a Passmark database operations score of 23567.62, both placing in the top 25%. However, single-core performance and memory write bandwidth remain average, and the lack of a GPU results in poor performance for large-scale LLM inference. This configuration is optimized for memory-heavy workloads, relational databases, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48bds_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48bds_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 1843 GB of local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 384 GB RAM, local storage, and strong multi-core capabilities for database and data-processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores
384 GB of system memory providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1843 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core performance and database operations in the top 25%
Poorly suited for large-scale LLM inference due to lack of GPU acceleration
Best fit for relational databases, memory-intensive applications, and data processing
